page: Amazon Web Services c5.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth. The server achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 95,252.05 and a PassMark CPU score of 63,325.12, placing it in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 5,275.1 GB/sec and large block uncached speeds at 235.82 GB/sec. It demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, achieving 82.96 million operations per second in Redis SET tests, and static web serving throughput of 47.05 GB/sec. However, single-core metrics and LLM text generation speeds are relatively modest, with text generation for a medium 7B model performing in the bottom 10% at 5.21 tokens per second.

Given its bare-metal architecture and high compute density, c5.metal offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require direct access to physical hardware resources without hypervisor overhead. The high vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly suitable for compute-intensive applications, software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput static web serving. Conversely, the lack of local storage and dedicated GPUs makes it less suitable for local storage-heavy databases or deep learning training workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c5.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs across 48 physical Intel Xeon 8275CL cores on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 3.6 GHz, it is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core processing and memory bandwidth, with a PassMark CPU score of 63,325.12 and a Redis SET performance of 82.96 million operations per second. While single-core performance and LLM text generation are average to poor, the server excels in multi-threaded tasks, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving. It is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, database operations, and applications requiring direct hardware access without hypervisor overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5.metal is a bare-metal compute-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps bandwidth, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring ninety-six vCPUs and high-bandwidth memory for demanding multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 3.6 GHz
192 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth
Bare-metal access with 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong multi-core CPU and database operation benchmark results
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and compute-heavy tasks
